message:
mcp: make CompatRuntime / CompatRuntimeBuilder imports non-test in mcp.rs

Prior commit moved these imports under `#[cfg(test)]` on the grounds
that the only non-test caller had switched to
`asupersync::runtime::RuntimeBuilder`. That was half-right: there is
still a non-test consumer of `CompatRuntime` / `CompatRuntimeBuilder`
elsewhere in this module (the mission-control / rules-list synchronous
scaffold builds on the compat runtime on tokio builds), so gating the
import to `#[cfg(test)]` breaks non-test compilation.

Fix: move the
`use crate::runtime_compat::{CompatRuntime, RuntimeBuilder as CompatRuntimeBuilder};`
line back up into the always-compiled import block, directly above
the `pub use mcp_bridge::{build_server, ...}` re-export, and drop the
duplicated `#[cfg(test)]`-gated copy that was sitting next to the
test-only `mcp_types::{EventsParams, ...}` import.

Test compilation is unaffected — the test module shares the same
imports now that they're unconditional.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
